type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Many changes were made to get the dust emissions to be more inclusive.  These include adding emissions from a baghouse, crusher scrubber, road dust, and dust off the tailings dam.  As well, the fugitive dust sources are better quantified (moving material from ground to mill).  Other changes include the Copper Dryer 2 being removed and replaced with a copper press and the domes on the stockpiles being completed.
 
Particulate matter was not reported in the past as we felt we were unable to get a reasonable estimate of the dust emissions.  The factors limiting this are that the majority of the dust on site is generated from roads travels by our light vehicle fleet versus our haul truck fleet.  The roads that the haul trucks travel on are watered frequently to reduce dust.  However, the light vehicle fleet travels on road not watered as frequently and therefore it appears that more dust is generate.  In looking at methodology provided by Environment Canada, we are unable to estimate this as we have no way of tracking the kilometers these trucks travel on dirt roads versus paved road.  As well, the silt content of the roads was measured and across our large property the silt content varied widely (6.4% - 33.4%).  In the 2011 reporting however, it was determined that we should report, even though it may not be representative of what is actually happening.  We therefore reported the particulate matter based on our haul truck fleet as we had sufficient information to complete the emissions factor estimates.
